notice: Maintenance & failover truth: every notice declares whether it is a maintenance, drain, failover, or tenant-migration window, the exact window time / timezone / offset, the affected deployment scope and write classes, which queued publish-later or local-draft work survives (kept separate from successful hosted mutations), what stays local-safe, and any changed tenant / region / endpoint boundary. A notice reads as current only while it is active and its last refresh is current — otherwise it downgrades and names why, and never collapses into a generic degraded banner. Shell, activity history, CLI / headless inspect, diagnostics, and support exports read this record verbatim.
title: Regional failover to the standby region
summary: Managed writes failed over to the standby region; the region and endpoint changed.

affected_scope.scope_summary: Managed-cloud control plane failed over from EU-central to EU-west.

axes.summary: Region moved to the standby region.
Endpoint identity changed with the failover.

boundary_change.summary: Region and endpoint identity changed; review before the next publish.

blocked_writes.note: A lifecycle write in flight at cutover needs a manual rerun after review.
Immediate publishes wait until the new region boundary is reviewed.

retained_local_safe_capabilities: Editing, saving, and local search continue.
Cached reads remain inspectable with a freshness label.
local_continuity.continuity_summary: Local work continues; managed writes wait for the new-region boundary review.

display_copy.scope_line: Managed-cloud control plane failed over from EU-central to EU-west.
display_copy.queued_preserved_line: 0 intents preserved (0 publish-later queued, 0 local drafts); they survive the window.

display_copy.local_continuity_line: Local work continues; managed writes wait for the new-region boundary review.
display_copy.boundary_change_line: Boundary change: 2 changed, 0 need recheck — Region and endpoint identity changed; review before the next publish.
display_copy.follow_up_line: Some writes need a manual rerun after the window.
